cards.examplePinyin: liǎng guó zhèng zài jìn xíng tán pàn
cards.note: Newsroom / boardroom register. For haggling over a price you'd reach for 讨价还价 (tǎo jià huán jià). Keep-it-handy chunk: 正在谈判 (zhèng zài tán pàn) — 'currently negotiating.'

cards.example: 数据中心给电网带来很大压力。
cards.examplePinyin: shù jù zhōng xīn gěi diàn wǎng dài lái hěn dà yā lì
cards.exampleMeaning: Data centers put enormous pressure on the power grid.
cards.note: 电 (diàn) = electricity — same 电 as 电脑 (diàn nǎo, 'electric brain' = computer). 网 (wǎng) = net/web, same 网 as the internet. Tone drill: 4th-tone falling 'pàn' vs 3rd-tone dipping 'wǎng' — that one contrast carries most beginner errors.

cards.meaning: a whirl, vortex, or turbulent churning — of wind, water, or emotion
cards.example: Sa gitna ng alimpuyo ng merkado, kailangan ng malamig na ulo.
cards.exampleMeaning: In the middle of the market's turbulence, keep a cool head.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance: heavier and more literary than 'ipo-ipo' (the small, almost playful whirlwind in the yard). 'Alimpuyo' is forceful, churning — the word for a super typhoon's fury or a chaotic market session. Common pairings: alimpuyo ng hangin (whirlwind), alimpuyo ng damdamin (turmoil of emotion).
cards.tiesTo: Super Typhoon Francisco / the market tape

cards.meaning: a conjecture; a reasoned supposition formed before the facts are in
cards.example: Sapantaha lang ito, ngunit malamang tama.
cards.exampleMeaning: This is only a conjecture, but it's likely correct.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance: dignified, unlike 'haka-haka,' which leans toward idle, unfounded speculation. 'Sapantaha' is a considered hypothesis — a thesis, not a rumor. One a hunch, the other a thesis: the sapantaha behind every rate-cut bet.
